text: This is the virtual bronchoscopy that you will use in the operating room to guide your bronchoscope and the working catheter down the correct bronchial segment.
You can see, based on this case report, that we are appropriately going to a posterior right upper lobe bronchial segment to get to his nodule.
You're using a therapeutic bronchoscope and the working catheter.
First you use a bronchoscopy view to guide the scope and the catheter as far as you can to the bronchial segment.

This video is not the patient in this case report, but is a representation of the VATS procedure used to locate the dye on the pleural surface and then use a stapling device to wedge resect the lesion.
One must take care to take adequate margins around the dye, as the nodule is not always in the dye itself, but often nearby.
This picture shows the patient in this case reports dye marking on his right upper lobe on the pleural surface.
And this is his resected specimen, which shows the dye and the lesion of interest.
The pathology on this lesion was an atypical adenomatous hyperplasia.
